mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, the discount rate on equities stays low and liquidity conditions support risk-taking, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive. The signal fails when rate cuts occur during recessionary demand collapse and earnings expectations deteriorate faster than discount rates.
failure_condition: If triggered periods with flat-to-falling FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than the unconditional baseline, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid CPIAUCSL acceleration above the recent trend forces expectations of tighter policy, raises real-rate pressure, and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when inflation rises from supply shocks but policy remains anchored and FEDFUNDS does not reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if CPI triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect disappears in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which leads investors to cut earnings forecasts and price lower equities over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if unemployment rises only after markets have already fully discounted a recession and policy easing offsets the shock.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If unemployment-rising episodes are not followed by below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is not negative, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates strengthening payroll income and retail demand, which improves near-term revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when employment gains are concentrated in low-wage sectors while sales are flat, or when demand is already saturated and valuations dominate.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS strength does not outperform either series alone in explaining 20d SPX returns, or if positive joint-sign periods fail to beat the unconditional return rate.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with improving HOUST signals that supply-side output and housing construction are recovering together, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when industrial output rises because of inventory restocking without final demand, or when higher mortgage rates suppress housing despite strong production.
failure_condition: If synchronized improvements in INDPRO and HOUST do not predict above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant in walk-forward tests, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in GDPC1 growth below trend signals fading aggregate demand, which lowers forward earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days. The hypothesis fails when GDP slows only because imports or inventories normalize while domestic demand remains resilient.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-slowdown triggers are followed by non-negative 60d SPX returns in most cases, or if the signal loses directional power in any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, the labor market is simultaneously creating jobs and absorbing slack, which boosts consumer confidence and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails if payroll gains are noisy revisions rather than real hiring, or if falling unemployment reflects labor-force exit instead of stronger demand.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either variable alone for 5d SPX returns, or if positive-return frequency under the trigger is not materially above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, if FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, investors anticipate further tightening and lower liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when high rates coincide with rapidly decelerating inflation, because the market then prices a policy pivot instead of sustained restraint.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation above 4%
failure_condition: If high-rate/high-inflation episodes are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if a policy-pivot interpretation dominates in rolling backtests, reject this hypothesis.
